title: Eugenia vahlii
intro: Eugenia vahlii, also known as the Vahlia Eugenia, is a deciduous shrub native to the Himalayan region. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Eugenia vahlii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Eugenia vahlii is commonly prepared as a decoction or powder, and it is also used to make various formulations such as syrups, capsules, and tablets.
taxonomy: Eugenia vahlii is classified under the family Myrtaceae. This family includes shrubs and trees commonly known as the myrtle family.
